signal_title: Industry Standards for Tackling Harmful Online Content
risk_rationale: The new Australian regulatory guidance requires tech companies to address harmful online content, which aligns with TikTok's existing content moderation policies but may require enhancements to meet specific Australian standards.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: CSAM detection using PhotoDNA + proprietary classifiers
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Address harmful online content, including child sexual abuse material
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The regulation may require additional measures or specific compliance mechanisms beyond current CSAM detection capabilities.

remediations.title: Enhance CSAM Detection and Reporting
remediations.description: Implement additional measures to comply with Australian standards for addressing harmful online content, potentially including enhanced detection algorithms or reporting mechanisms.

business_impact: Failure to comply with the new standards could result in regulatory action against TikTok in Australia, impacting user trust and platform reputation.
recommended_actions: Review and update content moderation policies to align with Australian standards
Enhance detection and reporting mechanisms for harmful content
Engage with Australian regulators to ensure compliance
